references.title: Improving learning experiences in software engineering capstone courses using artificial intelligence virtual assistants

extracted.Target of Recommendation: Lessons deemed useful for learner’s current Capstone Project
extracted.Data sources:
Lessons learned by previous students.

Lessons are represented by Emdeddings.

There is no representation of the users. Instead it is solely based on the content provided.
extracted.Updates to Recommendations: Recommendations for a given query are only changed aftert model retraining.
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Performed at the ESP level: html parsing, tonization and stopword removal.

Embeddings are created using Mulltilingual BERT.

Feedback on the interaction  between users and content is given by rating.
extracted.Evaluation: {Relevancy: 3.28 - Transformer, Transparency: 3.77 - Transformer, Novelty: 3.34 - Transformer, Serendipity: 3.12 - Transformer)
extracted.Comments:
Authors present Kanban, an educational platform whose aim is to assist in the development of a capstone project. 

Authors perform 2 distinct experiments with learners: 

1 - 4 Consultations on  Course Contents on the RS

2- Interact with the Chatbot that is meant to go beyond what the simple RS does.


Results on student ratings highlight that there are statistically signifcant differences between the proposed transformer model and the LSI baseline (except serendipity).
extracted.Limitations:
- Cannot use external sources of knowledge, 

- No analysis of pending tasks,

annotations.text: Students in Sofware Engineering Capstone Courses are usually recommended to record their learnings. However, students of future iterations usually are unable to take full advantage of these recordings.
The study tries to bridge the gap by combining recommender systems with Chatbot AI assistants. Experiments are conducted but the results are not mentioned in the abstract.
